author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
ore mana dhikre tomāya
pāiyā manuṣya janma, nā cintile kṛṣṇa karma,
vṛthā janma gelare khelāya

kateka sukṛti⋅phale, mānuṣa uttama kule,
tāhāte bhāratavarṣe janma
dhanya kaliyuga tā’te, śrī⋅kṛṣṇacaitanya jā’te,
prakāśilā nāma mātra dharma

pāye dhari chāḍa bhrama, kichu nāhi pariśrama,
kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa kaha avirāma
kaha lakṣa kathā āna, tāhe nā āsila jñāna,
ki bhāra ki bojhā kṛṣṇa⋅nāma

e jadi nā śuna bhāi, tabe āra gati nāi,
hena janma nā hoibe āra
kahe premānanda ebe, nā bhaja śrī⋅kṛṣṇa tabe,
koṭi kalpe nāhika nistāra
en_translation:
O my mind! Shame on you! Having received a human birth, you did not think of the service of Lord Kṛṣṇa; your life passed in vain in mere play.

Due to the results of many pious deeds, you were born as a human in a noble family, and furthermore, you were born in the sacred land of Bhārata. Blessed is this Age of Kali, where Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya revealed the religious practice of only the Holy Name.

I beg you, give up your illusion—there is no great effort involved. Just constantly chant "Kṛṣṇa! Kṛṣṇa!" You speak a million other things, but no knowledge comes from them. What is the burden, what is the weight of Kṛṣṇa's Name?

If you do not listen to this, O brother, then there is no other path. Such a birth will not come again. Śrīla Premānanda Dāsa says: "If you do not worship Lord Kṛṣṇa now, then even in millions of cosmic ages there will be no liberation."
